post.description: Merges a source ingredient into a target ingredient. This operation will move or merge stock items from source to target ingredient, optionally update recipes to use the target ingredient, optionally archive the source ingredient, and create an audit log entry. Stock items with the same quantity and unit will have their supplier packages merged. Stock items with different quantities or units will be moved to the target ingredient.

source_ingredient.description: The ID of the ingredient to be merged (source)

target_ingredient.description: The ID of the ingredient to merge into (target)

archive_source_ingredient.description: Whether to archive the source ingredient after merge

update_recipes_to_use_target_ingredient.description: Whether to update recipes to use the target ingredient after merge

move_stock.description: Whether to move stock items from source to target ingredient

stock_items_to_merge.description: Specific stock items to merge (if not provided, all stock items are merged)

stock_items_not_to_merge.description: Stock items to exclude from merge

post.description: Reverts a previously completed ingredient merge. This operation will move recipes back from target to source ingredient, unarchive the source ingredient, revert stock changes (optional), and mark the merge as reverted.

revert_stock.description: Whether to revert stock quantities to their pre-merge state

get.description: Returns financial data (food cost breakdown, profit margins, target/actual sell prices, ingredients cost) for a given recipe and outlet.

parameters.name: recipe_id
parameters.description: The ID of the recipe.

parameters.name: outlet
parameters.description: The ID of the outlet to calculate financials for.

parameters.name: food_cost_mode
parameters.description: The food cost calculation mode. One of: `cheapest` (default), `last_ordered`, `average_stock_value`.

id.description: The ID of the recipe.

name.description: The recipe name in the user's preferred language.

internal_uid.description: The internal UID of the recipe.

food_cost_mode.description: The food cost calculation mode used for this response.

tax.description: The tax percentage applied to the recipe. Returned with 3 decimal places.

servings.description: The number of servings for the recipe.

total_food_cost.description: Total food cost per serving (ingredients + waste).

prime_cost.description: Prime cost per serving (food cost + personnel cost).

target_for_one_serving.description: Target financial figures for one serving based on the configured profit margin.

profit_margin.description: Target profit margin percentage. Returned with 3 decimal places.

sell_price_excl_tax.description: Target sell price per serving excluding tax.

sell_price_incl_tax.description: Target sell price per serving including tax.

prime_cost_margin.description: Prime cost as a percentage of the sell price. Returned with 3 decimal places.

profit_by_actual_sell_price.description: Profit calculated using the actual sell price. Null if no actual sell price is set.

actual_for_one_serving.description: Actual financial figures for one serving based on the configured actual sell price. Fields are null if no actual sell price is set.

sell_price_excl_tax.description: Actual sell price per serving excluding tax.

sell_price_incl_tax.description: Actual sell price per serving including tax.

tax.description: Tax amount on the actual sell price.

difference_for_one_serving.description: Difference between target and actual figures for one serving. Fields are null if no actual sell price is set.

sell_price_excl_tax.description: Difference in sell price excluding tax.

profit_given_actual_sell_price.description: Profit given the actual sell price.

ingredients.description: List of ingredients with their cost breakdown.

id.description: The ID of the ingredient.

type.description: The type of the component. One of: INGREDIENT, RECIPE.

name.description: The ingredient name in the user's preferred language.

quantity.description: The quantity of the ingredient as a numeric string.

calculated_cost.description: The calculated cost for this ingredient in the recipe.

internal_uid.description: The internal UID of the ingredient.

403.description: User lacks the "recipe financial details" permission.

404.description: Recipe not found, outlet not found, or outlet belongs to a different library.

get.description: Returns a list of incoming orders for a supplier outlet.

parameters.name: page
parameters.description: A page number within the paginated result set.

parameters.name: ordering
parameters.description: A field to order results by.

parameters.name: supplier_outlet
parameters.description: Filter by the internal supplier outlet ID fulfilling the order.

parameters.name: ordering_outlet
parameters.description: Filter by the outlet ID that placed the original purchase order.

parameters.name: control_number
parameters.description: Filter on an exact order number.

parameters.name: control_number__contains
parameters.description: Filter on a order number containing the specified string.

schema.default: Filter on an exact purchase order number.

parameters.name: po_number__contains
parameters.description: Filter on a purchase order number containing the specified string.

parameters.name: status
parameters.description: Filter by incoming order status.

parameters.name: status__in
parameters.description: Filter by multiple incoming order statuses. Comma-separated values.

parameters.name: ordered_by
parameters.description: Filter by the user ID who ordered the original purchase order.

parameters.name: delivery_closed
parameters.description: Filter on orders that are or are not accepting more deliveries.

parameters.name: ordered_on
parameters.description: Filter on the exact 'ordered on' date of the purchase order.

parameters.name: ordered_on__gt
parameters.description: Filter orders where the purchase order's 'ordered on' date is after the specified date.

parameters.name: ordered_on__lt
parameters.description: Filter orders where the purchase order's 'ordered on' date is before the specified date.

parameters.name: received_on
parameters.description: Filter on the exact 'received_on' date of the purchase order.

parameters.name: received_on__gt
parameters.description: Filter orders where the purchase order's 'received_on' date is after the specified date.

parameters.name: received_on__lt
parameters.description: Filter orders where the purchase order's 'received_on' date is before the specified date.

parameters.name: expected_delivery_date
parameters.description: Filter on the exact expected delivery date of the purchase order.

parameters.name: expected_delivery_date__gt
parameters.description: Filter orders where the purchase order's expected delivery date is after the specified date.

parameters.name: expected_delivery_date__lt
parameters.description: Filter orders where the purchase order's expected delivery date is before the specified date.

parameters.name: discount
parameters.description: Filter on the exact discount amount of the purchase order.

parameters.name: discount__gt
parameters.description: Filter orders where the purchase order's discount is greater than the specified amount.

parameters.name: discount__lt
parameters.description: Filter orders where the purchase order's discount is less than the specified amount.

parameters.name: sent_by
parameters.description: Filter by the user ID who sent the order.

parameters.name: sent_on
parameters.description: Filter on the exact 'sent on' date of the order.

parameters.name: sent_on__gt
parameters.description: Filter orders where the 'sent on' date is after the specified date.

parameters.name: sent_on__lt
parameters.description: Filter orders where the 'sent on' date is before the specified date.

parameters.name: has_updated_stock
parameters.description: Filter on whether the order has updated stock when sent.

post.description: Generate a demand forecast for an outlet.

parameters.name: outlet_id
parameters.description: An outlet's ID.

forecast_from.description: The date from which the forecast should be generated. It should be in the format YYYY-MM-DD. It can be any date but you may want to use future dates. It should be lower than the forecast_to date.

forecast_to.description: The date to which the forecast should be generated. It should be in the format YYYY-MM-DD. It can be any date but you may want to use future dates. It should be higher than the forecast_from date.

sales_period.description: The sales period that should be considered for the forecast. If LAST_WEEK is selected, the forecast will be generated based on the sales of the last week. If LAST_4_WEEKS is selected, the forecast will be generated based on the sales of the last 4 weeks. If YEAR_AGO is selected, the forecast will be generated based on the sales of the same period last year.So if the forecast_from date is 2025-01-01 and the forecast_to date is 2025-01-31, the sales of 2024-01-01 to 2024-01-31 will be considered.
